quince-crumble.name: Quince Crumble
instructions: Wash lemons under cold water and dry well. Thinly peel 2 lemons and juice them. Fill a pot with cold water; add lemon juice and peels.

Peel quinces and remove core. Cut into small pieces and immediately drop into the pot with lemon water, so they don't turn brown. Add cinnamon stick and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook until quinces are easily pierced with a knife, 30 to 40 minutes. Drain and discard peels and cinnamon stick.
Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).
Place cooked quinces, white sugar, and ground cinnamon into a large bowl. Zest and juice remaining lemon; add to quince mixture and mix until well combined. Spread mixture evenly into a baking dish.
Make topping: Combine flour, oats, brown sugar, butter, nutmeg, and salt in a medium bowl. Rub between your fingers to create a streusel topping. Sprinkle streusel evenly over fruit.
Bake in the preheated oven until crumble is lightly browned, about 1 hour.

quince-strudel-with-ice-cream-lezzet.name: Quince Strudel With Ice Cream
instructions: 1. Extract flour for dough into kneading container.
2. Add egg, sugar and liquid oil.
3. Azar is kneaded until it gets a softly consistency dough by adding water.
4. Cover the dough with a top-shaped buckle.
5. Listen until half an hour in the room heat.
6. Wash and drain the moon for inner material.
7. Peel the peel and rub it in the form of cube and smear it in the gale.
8. melt butter with sugar in the pan.
9. Pope up to 10 minutes by adding the flowers and grapes.
10. Take it from January, add the comb and leave it to the spark after tasting.
11. 11. Take the chopped dough on the sprinkled counter.
12. Open a round and fine dough with the help of Oklava.
12. August The mixture of Ayvalik is sprinkled with the help of spoon and evenly distributed on the dough.
14 July Wrap the dough from two ends and in the form of rolls.
15. Place the oily paper into the oven tray.
16. Bake for 20-25 minutes in the oven, which is pre-heated to 180 degrees.
17. September Spread the powder sugar on the oven and hot.
18. Serve warmly with a grated lemon peel according to your request and warm with whipped cream and vanilla ice cream.

quinoa-bean-salad-with-lots-of-dill.name: Quinoa-bean Salad With Lots Of Dill

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 1 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Bring 1 cup quinoa, rinsed, a generous pinch of kosher salt, and 2 cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until quinoa is tender and water is absorbed, 15–20 minutes. Remove from heat and let sit on stovetop, still covered, until ready to use.

Meanwhile, toast ½ cup raw pumpkin seeds (pepitas) in a dry small skillet over medium-low heat, tossing occasionally and reducing heat if seeds begin to pop, until golden brown in spots, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat, sprinkle with a pinch of salt, and let cool.

Finely grate zest of 1 lemon into a measuring glass or small jar; cut lemon in half and squeeze in juice. Add 1 shallot, finely chopped, ½ cup extra-virgin olive oil, ¼ cup apple cider vinegar, ¼ cup Dijon mustard, ¼ cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt, 2 tsp. sumac, 1½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp. Morton kosher salt, and 1 tsp. freshly ground pepper and whisk or cover and shake vigorously until emulsified, about 30 seconds.

Place two 15.5-oz. cans white beans (such as cannellini, Great Northern, or navy), rinsed, 1 bunch dill, chopped (about 2 cups), reserved quinoa, and dressing in a large bowl and toss gently to combine. Season salad with salt and pepper.

When ready to serve, scoop out a portion of salad and toss with baby arugula (figure a handful per person). Top with some toasted pumpkin seeds and drizzle with oil.
Do Ahead: Salad (without arugula and pumpkin seeds) can be made 3 days ahead; cover and chill. Pumpkin seeds can be toasted 3 days ahead; store airtight at room temperature.

quinoatmeal-with-apple-and-toasted-walnu.name: Quinoatmeal With Apple And Toasted Walnuts
instructions: The night before cooking, rinse quinoa thoroughly and drain. Combine oats and quinoa in a medium saucepan and cover with 1 cup boiling water.

In the morning, bring oats and quinoa to a boil. Stir in almond milk, salt, and cinnamon and reduce heat to low. Cook, stirring occasionally, until quinoa grains are tender, 8–10 minutes. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 350°. Toast walnuts on a small rimmed baking sheet, stirring occasionally, until fragrant and slightly darkened in color, about 4 minutes. Let cool slightly.
Top quinoatmeal with apple, walnuts, and more cinnamon, if desired. Drizzle with almond milk.
